observations.value: POST /orders with a repeated Idempotency-Key does not honour idempotency: two sequential POSTs to /orders with 'Idempotency-Key: fresh-key-abc' (card=4111111111111111) returned distinct order_ids ('A1005' and 'A1006') and 'idempotent: false' on both responses. The same pattern was observed with 'Idempotency-Key: test-key-123' (A1001 vs A1002) and 'Idempotency-Key: unique-key-xyz' (A1003 vs A1004). The README states the endpoint accepts an Idempotency-Key header, implying the same key should return the same order_id on repeat calls.

POST /cart/apply accumulates duplicate coupon entries in the 'active' list: applying coupon 'SAVE10' a second time (subtotal=40) returned {'active': ['SAVE10', 'SAVE10']} instead of deduplicating or rejecting the duplicate, indicating the active coupon list is not deduplicated across repeated applications.

The same pattern was observed with 'Idempotency-Key: test-key-123' (A1001 vs A1002) and 'Idempotency-Key: unique-key-xyz' (A1003 vs A1004). The README states the endpoint accepts an Idempotency-Key header, implying the same key should return the same order_id on repeat calls.
